title: Additional Stores permissions issue on NFS share
body:
/kind bug


**Description**

I am trying to use the additional stores feature in Podman, as documented in [https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/image-stores-podman](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/image-stores-podman). It seems there is some issue with the locks not being generated and/or used properly. Is it possible I am just missing some permissions setting for the locks? I tried to manually touch the lock file but it did not help much. The /projects directory is mounted via NFS and this is on an RHEL8.4 aarch64 system.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ mkdir /projects/containers/add-store2
$ vi ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
$ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
graphroot = "/tmp/my-user-graphroot"
driver = "overlay"

[storage.options]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
additionalimagestores = ["/projects/containers/add-store2"]

$ podman images
Error: error opening "/projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-images/images.lock": no such file or directory
$ chmod -R a+rx /projects/containers/add-store2
$ podman images
Error: error opening "/projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-images/images.lock": no such file or directory
$ podman info
Error: error opening "/projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-images/images.lock": no such file or directory
$ # I'll try to create the sub-dir and touch the images lock file
$ mkdir /projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-images
$ touch /projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-images/images.lock
$ podman images
Error: error retrieving label for image "e5df02c436858279600da64c8614547753be6baf6ef4fee082e9c76e2762151f": you may need to remove the image to resolve the error: error opening "/projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-layers/layers.lock": no such file or directory
$ chmod -R a+rx /projects/containers/add-store2
$ podman images
Error: error retrieving label for image "e5df02c436858279600da64c8614547753be6baf6ef4fee082e9c76e2762151f": you may need to remove the image to resolve the error: error opening "/projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-layers/layers.lock": no such file or directory
$ # I'll also manually create the layers lock file
$ mkdir /projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-layers
$ touch /projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-layers/layers.lock
$ podman images
REPOSITORY                       TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8  latest      eb537cb8064c  5 weeks ago   267 MB
quay.io/centos/centos            8           a0477e85b8ae  9 months ago  256 MB
docker.io/library/centos         7           e5df02c43685  9 months ago  310 MB
$ podman --root /projects/containers/add-store2  pull fedora
Error: lock "/projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-images/images.lock" is not a read-only lock
```

**Describe the results you received:**

I am getting the following error when trying to use additional stores:
Error: error opening "/projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-images/images.lock": no such file or directory

When I create the images.lock and layers.lock files manually, I still get the following error wile trying to use the additional store:
Error: lock "/projects/containers/add-store2/overlay-images/images.lock" is not a read-only lock


**Describe the results you expected:**

That `podman --root /projects/containers/add-store2 pull fedora` would work.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**


```
$ podman version
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Tue Jul 27 01:30:08 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

NOTE: hostname removed, username faked to "my-user", and $UID changed to fake "12345" for security reasons.

```
$ podman info --debug
host:
  arch: arm64
  buildahVersion: 1.21.3

  conmon:

    path: /usr/bin/conmon

  cpus: 112

  hostname: ##REMOVED##

    - container_id: 0

      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 12345
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 200000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 4.18.0-240.15.1.el8_3.aarch64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 267904942080
  memTotal: 273825923072
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: |-
      runc-1.0.0-70.rc92.module+el8.3.1+9857+68fb1526.aarch64
      runc-1.0.0-74.rc95.module+el8.4.0+11822+6cc1e7d7.aarch64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version spec: 1.0.2-dev
      go: go1.15.13
      libseccomp: 2.5.1
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/12345/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false

  swapFree: 4294901760

      Version: |-

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-2.2.1-7.module+el8.3.1+9857+68fb1526.aarch64
podman-3.2.3-0.10.module+el8.4.0+11989+6676f7ad.aarch64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


YES

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is running on a Marvell ThunderX2 aarch64 system with RHEL8.4.

```
$ uname -a
Linux ##REMOVED## 4.18.0-240.15.1.el8_3.aarch64 #1 SMP Wed Feb 3 03:16:05 EST 2021 aarch64 aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
I think you need to create the additional store as the root user, so it can use any other additional ID.

You'll also need to create the storage before setting it in the `additionalimagestores`, otherwise the storage tries to use it before it is ready.

Ok I tried to create a new directory /projects/containers/add-store3 as a privileged user and also ran ```chmod -R a+rx /projects/containers/add-store3``` but I still see that Podman doesn't know see the lock. Should I be manually creating these or would Podman presumably create them?

```
$ ls -l /projects/containers
total 68144
drwxrwxr-x  9 my-user my-user        4096 Sep  8 09:49 add-store2
drwxr-xr-x  2 root    root           4096 Sep  9 08:22 add-store3
...
$ ls -l add-store3
total 0
$ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
graphroot = "/tmp/my-user-graphroot"
driver = "overlay"

[storage.options]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
additionalimagestores = ["/projects/containers/add-store3"]
$ podman images
Error: error opening "/projects/containers/add-store3/overlay-images/images.lock": no such file or directory
```

@ajyounge is this issue still relevant?

Have you tried to create the entire storage with `podman --root /projects/containers/add-store3 pull foo` ?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have had no info in a month, I am going to close.  Reopen if this is still an issue and  you have more info.

I can confirm that either created as root or as a user, permissions are too restrictive for another user to use it in `additionalimagestores`. Is it possible to specify which permissions are used when creating the image store? And when updating it?
you can do it with `force_mask = "shared"` in `storage.conf`.  You also need to use a mount program and chown the storage directory itself

It does not seem to work with podman 3.4.4. Here's how I proceeded:

1. run as root: `install -d -o drone -g drone -m 755 /data/containers/images`
2. configured `/home/drone/.config/containers/storage.conf` like this:
```toml
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot = "/data/containers/images"

[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
force_mask = "shared"
```
3. run as drone: `podman pull alpine:3.14`\
4. checked permissions with ls:
```
drone@home:~> ls -al /data/containers/images/
total 37
drwxr-xr-x 9 drone drone 11 Mar 31 17:41 .
drwxr-xr-x 3 root  root   3 Mar 31 17:41 ..
drwx------ 2 drone drone  4 Mar 31 17:41 libpod
drwx------ 2 drone drone  2 Mar 31 17:41 mounts
drwx------ 4 drone drone  5 Mar 31 17:41 overlay
drwx------ 2 drone drone  3 Mar 31 17:41 overlay-containers
drwx------ 3 drone drone  5 Mar 31 17:41 overlay-images
drwx------ 2 drone drone  5 Mar 31 17:41 overlay-layers
-rw-r--r-- 1 drone drone 64 Mar 31 17:41 storage.lock
drwx------ 2 drone drone  2 Mar 31 17:41 tmp
-rw-r--r-- 1 drone drone  0 Mar 31 17:41 userns.lock
```

Same result with this config:

```toml
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot = "/data/containers/images"

[storage.options]
force_mask = 0o755

[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
force_mask = "shared"
```

Should I wait for openSUSE Tumbleweed to update to podman 4.0.2 ?

Thanks so much for your help though!

are the files under ` /data/containers/images/overlay` stored with the correct mask?

For the upper directories you'll need to manually tweak the permission masks with `chmod -R 755 /data/containers/images`

Nope, I still have to manually to `chmod -R 755 $REPO` every time an image is built. Here is the commands I ran:

1. as root:
```
root@home:~> chmod -R 755 /data/containers/images
```

2. as drone:
```
drone@home:~> podman pull python:3-alpine
Resolving "python" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf)

total 25
drwxr-xr-x 9 drone drone 10 Apr  1 08:25 .

-rwxr-xr-x 1 drone drone  4 Apr  1 08:25 .has-mount-program

total 41
```
